quote_of_day.text:
Damn the inevitable civil war is going.
Everyone has given up of democratic institutions either a strongman comes and rules or we balkanised.
Fuck i should get out of this hellhole before it becomes worse
quote_of_day.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather on the board today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of caste critique moved into the board's Leading section, bringing with it a squall of complaints about the Kshatriya caste's supposed failures to meet modern standards.

The system's leading edge was marked by claims of cowardice, nihilism, and a lack of physical and financial prowess.

In the Discourse section, a high-pressure ridge of technical discussion formed, with one anon announcing the alpha release of Indiachan Lite and calling for volunteers to test the site.

Meanwhile, another thread in the same section saw a gentle breeze of conversation about Induschan and IIT prestige.

A cold snap of personal reports chilled the Notices section, with one anon claiming to have never received a consensual hug and another being met with hostility after asking what someone did.

The temperature dropped further when one respondent was called Aryaflaggot.

articles.title: Anon Claims Kshatriya Caste Fails Modern Standards
articles.body:
Anon opened the thread by listing supposed shortcomings of a Kshatriya caste member, including lacking courage, being nihilistic, and not earning money. The post claimed these individuals fail to meet traditional Kshatriya standards in modern times.

Another anon responded by suggesting that a modern Kshatriya should embody the qualities of a 'Chad', being forward, athletic, and driven to achieve. This anon argued that Kshatriyas should be diametrically opposed to intellectual nihilism.

One respondent proposed that the concept of Savarna-hood still exists among India's elite, and that ancient rishis and sages would adapt their teachings to modern times if they were alive today.

This anon suggested that power dynamics remain a key aspect of Savarna status. The discussion turned to the topic of homosexuality, with one anon stating that a revival of Savarna values would eventually lead to acceptance of homosexuality.

This anon suggested that strong, developed Kshatriya men would be capable of bisexuality. Not all respondents agreed, with one stating that homosexuality is a sin and that individualism and independence can weaken families and society.

Another anon argued that homosexuality provides intelligence benefits, citing a supposed genetic link.

The conversation also touched on the idea of a synthesis between historical and modern values, with one anon suggesting that the spirit of Kshatriyahood remains relevant, but its form must adapt. Another respondent proposed that tasteful homosexuality could be developed within Savarna ranks.

The thread also saw criticism of traditional caste ideology, with one anon claiming that real Indian kings were Shudras, and that the concept of Kshatriyahood is only relevant in story tales.

articles.title: Anons Discuss Induschan and IIT Prestige
articles.body:
Anon kicked off the conversation with a simple 'Coming soon..'.

One anon responded by referencing Chanakya Neeti, stating that it's better not to live where a wealthy person, a highly versed Brahmin, a river, and some other things are present.

Another anon argued that people with an IIT background will always be viewed as smart, while a crorepati may not receive the same level of respect. Anon also started sharing their thoughts on IIT prestige.

One claimed that even if someone is not from IIT, they can still be tech-savvy, but won't receive the same respect. Another anon mentioned that someone might take a drop year to pursue Induschan.

The conversation also touched on the topic of Induschan's development and its potential to replace another platform. Anon expressed skepticism about Induschan's ability to avoid carrying over bad things from the previous platform.

Others discussed the development process and potential timelines. Some anons engaged in a lighthearted exchange about privilege and respect. One anon joked that it's time to become a 'csbhangi', while another discussed their experiences with exams.

The thread also included some technical discussions, with one anon sharing a link to induschan.site and another asking about server issues.

articles.title: Anon Announces Alpha Release of Indiachan Lite
articles.body:
Anon announced the upcoming alpha release of Indiachan Lite, a new version of the site. The poster mentioned that the codebase is old, having been used for almost 1.5 years of 'bharatchan abuse'.

Anon expressed hope that volunteers would help test the website, describing it as being in a raw state with no domain yet. One respondent asked if the new version would be more comfortable, and another inquired about the boards that would be included.

Some users discussed the potential inclusion of various boards, such as /sci/, /acd/, and /g/. One poster suggested that /g/ might not fit alongside the other two boards. Another user proposed combining boards to increase posts and 'kino'.

The conversation also touched on the topic of user engagement. One user mentioned that their threads used to get more replies on the old site, and now feels like nobody looks at their posts.

Another user shared a similar sentiment, expressing sadness about the perceived decline in user interaction. Other users made seemingly unrelated posts, including one sharing an image of Uncle Chips and another discussing getting a haircut. A more ominous post suggested creating explicit content.

The discussion also referenced an 'aryan exodus' and the potential exclusion of certain groups, with one user stating that a Hindi-only site would 'filter out dravidian abbos'.

articles.title: OP claims never received consensual hug
articles.body:
Anon opened the thread stating they had never received a consensual warm hug, let alone consensual sex, and that they had bought sex multiple times.

One respondent suggested that the OP likely received a hug during early childhood but didn't remember it. Another Anon questioned the need to be touched or keep count of physical interactions, saying it "means and proves nothing".

The thread took a joking turn, with some Anons engaging in banter and personal jabs. One Anon suggested the OP try hugging a stranger in a "rich privileged area".

The discussion remained lighthearted and off-topic, with some users making humorous comments about their own experiences with physical touch and relationships.

articles.title: Anon asks what OP does, gets called Aryaflaggot
articles.body: Anon started the thread by asking 'what do you do'. One anon replied that OP is 'some bimaru Brahmin' and possibly homosexual or bisexual. Another anon called OP 'Aryaflaggot'. Anon described OP as 'stocky' and not their type, preferring 'lanklets'. OP's height was discussed, with one anon saying 161 and another misreading it as 160.
